bottom_modal.notes: Only in Control; Control is 7-way confounded vs V1. Implicitly ruled out via its 1:1 tie to modal_interrupt=yes (which has 62% friction rate).

full_screen_dark.notes: Only in V1; V1 is 7-way confounded vs Control. Dark-theme-specific attribution impossible from this dataset.

full_screen.notes: Adopted by 3 of 4 post-Control variants. Implicitly safe. Direct attribution vs full_screen_dark not possible.

recommended.rationale: Universally adopted post-Control. Non-dark because dark is V1-only and confounded with 6 other changes.

yes.notes: 62% cross-segment flag rate. Resolved in every post-Control variant. Magnitude unknown (no clean contrast) but direction is unambiguous and high-prevalence.

no.notes: Universal adoption in post-Control variants. Implied positive by removal of cross-segment friction.

recommended.rationale: 62% of users flagged the modal as friction; every post-Control variant removed it.

none.notes: V4 (none) outperforms V3 (crown_header) overall, but V3->V4 is 5-way confounded — not attributable.

crown_header.confound_basis: Co-varies with trade_evidence=blurred_card and refund_or_guarantee_copy=implicit_refund across V2/V3.

corroborating_friction.note: Attributes to crown_header + low_contrast_subordinate combo, not crown_header alone.

recommended.rationale: No clean contrast; crown_header is confounded across every variant it appears in. Safer default: none, supported by V4's performance (with caveat that V3->V4 attribution is confounded).

opaque.notes: 78% flag rate. Decisive cross-segment negative. Resolved in every post-Control variant.

visible_primary.notes: Present in both the highest-overall (V4, 44%) and Trust-Seeker-peaking (V1, 60% on Trust) variants. Can't separate from co-varying changes.

visible_with_framing.confound_basis: Perfectly co-varies with refund_or_guarantee_copy=implicit_refund — likely two names for the same on-screen element.

recommended.rationale: NOT opaque (78% friction — decisive). Between visible_primary and visible_with_framing, the latter is confounded with refund copy; visible_primary has cleaner evidence of being non-harmful.

cta_primary_label._note: Freeform strings, not an enumeration. Ranking is qualitative.

Start Trial Now.notes: Control-only. Supporting quote: "Trial without a price means hidden charge" — but the friction attributes to opaque price, not the label.

Activate for ₹1.notes: V2/V3 is inferred (extraction_confidence=inferred). Used across three variants with dramatically different outcomes (V1=38% vs V2=30%). The label itself is not rank-informative — its performance tracks whatever else varies.

Unlock FREE trade.notes: V4-only. Associated friction (dual_cta_label_mismatch, 10%) actually attributes to cta_stack=dual_outline_plus_sticky, not the label itself. But IDEA.md Skeptical quote flags the framing as 'mildly deceptive' in 33% of V4 users (element-level). Direction: the word FREE creates an expectation the ₹1 flow breaks.

See 1 real trade, free.expected_mechanism: Overlay proposal: honest framing. Removes 'Unlock FREE trade' -> ₹1 label mismatch. Expected to reduce the 33% mismatch-notice rate, improving Skeptical Investor trust.
See 1 real trade, free.pairs_with: trade_evidence=real_closed_trade (they share the 'show a real thing' honesty mechanism).

recommended.rationale: The only label with an explicit mechanism to close the trust gap opened by 'Unlock FREE -> ₹1' mismatch. Untested — synthesizer must flag as such.

skeptical_investor.basis: V2 minus V3 delta (this side of the contrast)

trust_seeker.basis: V2 minus V3 delta — Trust Seeker PREFERS low_contrast over green

corroborating_friction.note: 22% missed the CTA entirely or mis-tapped banner — attributes to this value's subordinate positioning.

low_contrast_subordinate.notes: NEGATIVE overall (−6.42pt) but POSITIVE for Trust Seeker (+10pt vs green). If Trust Seeker is the dominant segment, this value wins. Important counter-intuitive result.

trust_seeker.basis: V3 minus V2 delta — Trust Seeker penalty

contradictions_applied.note: Trust Seeker −10pts is already in per_segment_impact; not double-applied.

corroborating_friction.note: 40% of Trust Seekers flagged as reducing premium feel; corroborates the −10pt in the contrast.

high_contrast_green.notes: +6.42pt weighted score is the ONLY clean contrast-derived number in this matrix. Net positive but carries a real Trust Seeker cost. Magnitude of Bargain +16pt is well above noise band; Trust −10pt is at the edge of noise (n=10) but corroborated by friction.

high_contrast_warm.expected_mechanism: Similar conversion boost to green for Bargain/Curious, unknown Trust effect. No observation data.

high_contrast_cool.expected_mechanism: Between neutral and green — may split the difference. No data.

muted_premium.expected_mechanism: Dark teal. Preserve the +16/+7/+9 pt lift for Bargain/Curious/Skeptical while removing the −10pt Trust Seeker penalty that bright green triggers (premium-feel mechanism). Expected weighted score upper bound: +8.42pt (same positives + Trust preserved at 0). Untested — requires explicit flag in synthesize.
muted_premium.expected_contradiction: none inherited from green+Trust (the mechanism we're avoiding).
muted_premium.pairs_with: branding=none (no crown), trust_signal=regulatory_plus_evidence (restore V1 trust markers).

alternative_for_audience_skew.trust_seeker_heavy: muted_premium (untested) — expected to preserve gains and remove Trust penalty
alternative_for_audience_skew.trust_seeker_dominant_over_40pct: low_contrast_subordinate — the V2 cta_style — observationally Trust-Seeker-preferred

recommended.rationale: +6.42pt weighted score on current audience (24/30/26/20). Clean contrast, corroborating friction, highest-confidence decision in this dataset. If synthesize wants to eliminate the Trust Seeker penalty, reach for muted_premium with explicit untested flag.

single.notes: Adopted by 4 of 5 variants. Implicitly safe baseline.

friction_evidence._element_level_note: 33% of V4 users noticed the discrepancy — element-level signal, more prevalent than the 10% friction flag count.

dual_outline_plus_sticky.notes: V3->V4 overall +7pt but 5-way confounded. Dual-stack's own contribution not attributable. The 33% mismatch-notice rate is a trust cost not reflected in headline conversion.

recommended.rationale: Observationally safe. Dual stack has explicit friction (33% notice the mismatch) with unclear upside — the V3->V4 lift comes from multiple simultaneous changes.
recommended.alternative: primary_plus_secondary_link (untested) — may preserve the two-option optionality without the mismatch problem

none.notes: Adopted by 4 of 5 variants. Implied safe.

countdown_timer.notes: 50% of Skeptical Investors flagged as manufactured urgency. No clean contrast to establish pts magnitude. Removed in V2-V4 (variant-wide decision post-V1). Direction: strong negative for Skeptical Investor specifically.

scarcity_count.expected_contradiction: Likely inherits countdown_timer's Skeptical-Investor alienation — same manipulative-urgency family.

deadline_text.notes: Static text deadline may avoid the 'ticking' manipulation cue but remains speculative.

recommended.rationale: V1 evidence shows countdown_timer alienates 50% of Skeptical Investors. Scarcity/social-proof alternatives inherit the same mechanism risk. None is the observed-safe choice.

absent.notes: V4 (absent) is the highest-overall variant, but V3->V4 is 5-way confounded. Also a V4 Skeptical quote references 'refund' despite this value being absent per source — possible source gap (see matrix.flags).

implicit_refund.confound_basis: Perfectly co-varies with price_visibility=visible_with_framing. Likely same on-screen element.
implicit_refund.notes: 64% of users cited refund clause as strongest pricing reassurance (element-level), but can't separate from the price framing.

explicit_sla.expected_mechanism: Overlay proposal: 'Refund in 60s to source. No questions.' Closes the Skeptical Investor trust gap with concrete SLA. Expected strongest lift on Skeptical (+3-5pts qualitative) given refund mention was cited by 64% element-level.
explicit_sla.pairs_with: trade_evidence=real_closed_trade (both are concreteness mechanisms for Skeptical)

recommended.rationale: 64% of users cited refund as strongest reassurance. Making it explicit with a named SLA removes the Skeptical trust gap without adding friction. Untested.

friction_evidence.note: Attributes to 'implicit + no evidence_detail' — cost of dropping from V1's regulatory_plus_evidence.

implicit.notes: Post-V1 default. The friction signal (16% cross-segment) documents what dropping V1's trust markers costs.

regulatory_plus_evidence.confound_basis: Perfectly co-varies with evidence_detail=named_past_outcome — both appear only in V1. Also confounded by 5 other V1-only changes vs Control.

corroborating_signal.element_level: 71% cited a stock by name in V1. Trust Seeker peaked at 60% (highest in dataset). Curious Beginner +20pt vs Control (implied via named_wins anchor, e.g., ZOMATO quote).

regulatory_plus_evidence.notes: Observationally the best performer on Trust Seeker and Curious Beginner, but attribution between regulatory and evidence is impossible from this dataset alone.

regulatory.expected_mechanism: SEBI number alone without named wins. Likely captures Trust Seeker baseline (compliance reassurance) but loses the Curious Beginner anchor.

evidence_mode.expected_mechanism: Named wins without regulatory. May lose Trust Seeker compliance anchor.

recommended.rationale: Best-observed performer for Trust Seeker and Curious Beginner. Single-attribution impossible from dataset, but V1's 60% Trust peak + Curious +20pt lift are real.

named_past_outcome.confound_basis: Always co-occurs with trust_signal=regulatory_plus_evidence in this dataset.
named_past_outcome.element_level_note: 71% cited a stock by name — direct evidence of cognitive anchoring.

aggregate_metric.expected_mechanism: Per IDEA.md: '84.7% accuracy' claim was Skeptical-dismissed (V4 quote: 'I still don't believe the 84.7% claim'). Likely inferior to named_past_outcome for Skeptical Investor.

real_outcome_disclosure.expected_mechanism: Full disclosure (entry/exit/days/gain). Stronger signal than named_past_outcome for Skeptical Investor; removes the 'can I verify this?' trust gap.

recommended.alternative: real_outcome_disclosure (untested) — stronger mechanism for Skeptical Investor
recommended.rationale: 71% cited a stock by name. Dropping this in V2-V4 is the 16% cross-segment friction 'abstract_metrics_vs_named_wins'.

friction_evidence.note: 100% of Skeptical Investors flagged. Persistent across V2/V3/V4. Highest single-segment friction rate in the dataset.

supporting_quote.quote: Show me a real trade or don't

blurred_card.notes: Unambiguous negative for Skeptical Investor (100% flag rate, persistent across 3 variants). Magnitude of conversion impact unknown (no clean contrast) but direction and prevalence are decisive.

real_closed_trade.expected_mechanism: Fully disclosed closed trade (entry/exit/days/gain). Removes the blurred_card trust gap (the 100%-flagged Skeptical friction). Expected to close the +8-10pt gap between V4 Skeptical (25%) and V1-equivalent Skeptical-oriented design.

recommended.rationale: blurred_card has 100% Skeptical flag rate across 3 variants — decisive negative. The overlay-proposed alternative (real_closed_trade) is the only candidate with a mechanism that removes the friction entirely. Untested — synthesizer must flag.

dimension_summary.note: Only cta_style has a clean-contrast-derived pts score (+6.42 for high_contrast_green vs low_contrast_subordinate). 5 more dimensions have decisive direction via high-prevalence friction (modal_interrupt, price_visibility, urgency_mechanism, trade_evidence) or universal post-Control adoption (layout). The other 6 dimensions require untested-value exploration for meaningful V5 uplift.

flags: Sole clean-contrast-derived pts score is cta_style's +6.42. Every other weighted recommendation leans on friction direction, universal adoption, or untested expected-mechanism — not observed pts.

V4.refund_or_guarantee_copy is source-flagged as absent but V4 Skeptical quote mentions 'refund' (see matrix.flags). If source is wrong, refund_or_guarantee_copy recommendation shifts — blocker for V5.
Small-sample uncertainty: Trust Seeker n=10, so -10pt delta for high_contrast_green is at the edge of noise. Treat as directionally real but wide-interval.

Three dimensions' recommendations are untested values (cta_primary_label, refund_or_guarantee_copy, trade_evidence). synthesize must widen the prediction interval for any V5 that adopts multiple untested values simultaneously.
